goal: Land per-branch LanceDB vector-store lifecycle in a NEW `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` module — a `BranchManager` that owns the `<base>/branches/<branch>/vectors/` LanceDB connection root per master-plan §11.2 line 1074 and §12.2 lines 1321-1346, exposing three async lifecycle operations: (a) `create_branch_table(name, parent: Option<&str>)` opens a `lancedb::connect()` at the per-branch `vectors/` directory, creates the `code_chunks` empty table with the master-plan §12.2 schema (id/file_path/start_line/end_line/content/language/symbol_name/symbol_kind/embedding[768]/token_count/file_hash/indexed_at), and on `parent = Some(other)` performs a delta-clone by recursively copying the parent branch's `vectors/` directory tree before opening the connection (so the new branch starts with the parent's already-indexed chunks per master-plan §6.4 'delta indexing from parent branches for fast creation'); (b) `archive_branch_table(name)` renames `<base>/branches/<sanitised>/vectors/` to `<base>/branches/.archive/<sanitised>-<unix_ts_micros>/vectors/` so the table data persists for forensics but the live tree is clean (master-plan §6.4 'creates, updates, prunes, and archives per-branch code indexes'); (c) read-only accessors `branch_vectors_dir(name)` and `archive_root()` for path arithmetic. Frozen acceptance test `branch_manager::test_lancedb_per_branch` lands at MODULE ROOT of `branch_manager.rs` per DEC-0007, exercises 5 sub-assertions covering full lifecycle (create root branch / open table / clone-from-parent / archive / archive-roundtrip-table-readable). Cite master-plan §3.2 line 1643 ('branch_manager.rs' lives in `ucil-daemon/src/`), §6.4 line 144 ('Branch index manager: Creates, updates, prunes, and archives per-branch code indexes. Delta indexing from parent branches for fast creation'), §11.2 line 1074 ('vectors/' under per-branch), §12.2 lines 1321-1346 (LanceDB code_chunks schema), §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1782 ('LanceDB per-branch') verbatim in the rustdoc + commit body.
plan_summary: Create NEW file `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` (~280-340 LOC including doc + impl + test, 5 commits per the ladder below). Add `lancedb`, `arrow-array`, `arrow-schema` workspace deps to `Cargo.toml` at workspace level (alphabetical insertion in the `[workspace.dependencies]` section); add `lancedb.workspace = true` and the two arrow crates to `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml [dependencies]`. The `BranchManager` is constructed via `BranchManager::new(branches_root: PathBuf)` (where `branches_root = <base>/branches/`); it stores the root + the archive subdirectory (`<branches_root>/.archive/`) as `PathBuf` fields. `code_chunks_schema()` is a private free function that returns the `Arc<arrow_schema::Schema>` for the §12.2 schema (12 fields, embedding is a `FixedSizeList(Float32, 768)` per CodeRankEmbed default). `create_branch_table` does (1) sanitise via `sanitise_branch_name` (mirror of `storage::sanitise_branch` — `/` → `-`); (2) if `parent.is_some()`, recursively copy `<branches_root>/<sanitised_parent>/vectors/` to `<branches_root>/<sanitised_name>/vectors/`; (3) `tokio::fs::create_dir_all` the target vectors dir (no-op if cloned); (4) `lancedb::connect(uri).execute().await` where `uri = vectors_dir.to_str().expect(...)` — but the `.expect` is forbidden outside tests, so use `to_str().ok_or(BranchManagerError::NonUtf8Path { .. })?`; (5) `conn.table_names().execute().await?` to check if `code_chunks` already exists (delta-clone case — table is already populated), and if absent call `conn.create_empty_table('code_chunks', code_chunks_schema()).execute().await?`. `archive_branch_table` does (1) sanitise; (2) compute `archive_target = archive_root.join(format!('{sanitised}-{ts}', ts = std::time::SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap_or_default().as_micros()))`; (3) `tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&self.archive_root).await?`; (4) `tokio::fs::rename(branch_dir, &archive_target).await?` — tokio's rename is atomic on the same filesystem; the whole branch directory moves (vectors/ + symbols.db + tags.lmdb + state.json), preserving cross-table consistency. `BranchManagerError` is a `thiserror::Error` enum with variants `NonUtf8Path { path }`, `Lance { source: lancedb::Error }`, `Arrow { source: arrow_schema::ArrowError }`, `Io { source: std::io::Error }`, `ParentNotFound { parent }`, `BranchNotFound { name }` (`#[non_exhaustive]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`). The test `test_lancedb_per_branch` is `#[tokio::test]` at module root of `branch_manager.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007, exercises 5 sub-assertions in order: (SA1) `mgr.create_branch_table('main', None).await.expect()` → `branches_root.join('main/vectors').exists()` AND `lancedb::connect(...).execute().await?.table_names().execute().await? contains 'code_chunks'`; (SA2) clone-from-parent: `mgr.create_branch_table('feat/foo', Some('main')).await.expect()` → child dir `branches_root.join('feat-foo/vectors').exists()` AND child connection lists `code_chunks` (table preserved through directory copy); (SA3) sanitisation invariant: `branches_root.join('feat/foo')` does NOT exist (raw slashed path), only `feat-foo` exists; (SA4) archive roundtrip: `mgr.archive_branch_table('feat/foo').await.expect()` → `branches_root.join('feat-foo')` does NOT exist AND `branches_root.join('.archive')` exists AND contains a child whose name starts with `feat-foo-` (timestamp suffix); (SA5) archive-side connectability: open `lancedb::connect()` on the archive's `vectors/` dir AND assert `code_chunks` table is still listed (so archived data is forensically queryable, not just a tarball). Add `pub mod branch_manager;` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (alphabetical between `pub mod auto_indexing` if exists OR at the existing alphabetical position before `pub mod executor;` — actual position: BETWEEN line 97 and `pub mod executor;` at line 98, since 'b' < 'e' alphabetically) and a NEW re-export line covering all 5 new public symbols (`BranchManager`, `BranchManagerError`, `code_chunks_schema`, `BranchTableInfo`, `ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME`) per the WO-0042→WO-0048 cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline (now 9 consecutive WOs cleared on this discipline). Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F09.sh` per the standing convention. Pre-bake 3 mutation entries (literal sed AND runtime-only variant per WO-0046 lessons line 245): (M1) `create_branch_table` body neutered to early `Ok(())` return → SA1 panics because vectors dir was not created; (M2) `archive_branch_table` body neutered to early `Ok(())` → SA4 panics because branch dir is still in place and archive is empty; (M3) parent-clone branch (the `if parent.is_some()` block) neutered to skip the directory copy → SA2 panics because child connection cannot list `code_chunks` (table never propagated from parent).
scope_in: Add to `Cargo.toml` workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` section, alphabetically-correct positions: `lancedb = { version = "0.10", default-features = false }` (no GPU / no Python features — pure Rust async API), `arrow-array = "52"`, `arrow-schema = "52"`. Pinning to a known-stable line: lancedb 0.10.x is the most-recent stable as of 2026-Q1 with the `Connection::create_empty_table` + `Connection::table_names` + `lancedb::connect` async API surface. If the executor finds the version unavailable, pick the closest stable ≥ 0.4 (ABI-stable lineage) and document the choice in the commit body. The executor MUST verify the workspace builds via `cargo build -p ucil-daemon --quiet` before proceeding. The `default-features = false` is critical — full features pull in Python bindings, GPU search backends, and a multi-GB compile graph; ucil-daemon only needs the async Rust client.

Add to `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml [dependencies]` (alphabetical insertion): `arrow-array.workspace = true`, `arrow-schema.workspace = true`, `lancedb.workspace = true`. Existing entries (`thiserror.workspace = true`, `tokio.workspace = true`, etc.) MUST keep their alphabetical position; insert the new lines accordingly. AC02 verifies via `grep -E '^lancedb\.workspace = true' crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`.

Create NEW file `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` (~280-340 LOC including doc + impl + 1 test). Module-level `//!` rustdoc preamble cites master-plan §3.2 line 1643, §6.4 line 144, §11.2 line 1074, §12.2 lines 1321-1346, §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1782 verbatim. The preamble explains the three lifecycle operations (`create_branch_table`, `archive_branch_table`, accessor methods), the `delta clone via directory recursive-copy` design choice (preserves parent's already-populated `code_chunks` table; subsequent indexing only re-processes changed files per the file_hash column on the schema), the per-branch isolation guarantee (one LanceDB connection per `<base>/branches/<branch>/vectors/`, no cross-branch table sharing), and the archive convention (`<base>/branches/.archive/<sanitised>-<unix_ts_micros>/`). Include a `# Examples` block on the public methods per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` — the example may use `tokio::runtime::Runtime::new()` for synchronous doctest execution.

Declare `pub const ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME: &str = ".archive";` at module root (the `.` prefix matches POSIX hidden-dir convention; the literal is referenced from `BranchManager::new` and the test SA4). Rustdoc cites master-plan §6.4 line 144 and notes that the prefix is intentional (filesystem listings tooling that respects hidden-dir conventions skips it by default).

Declare `pub fn code_chunks_schema() -> std::sync::Arc<arrow_schema::Schema>` returning the master-plan §12.2 schema as 12 `arrow_schema::Field` entries: `id` (Utf8, non-nullable), `file_path` (Utf8, non-nullable), `start_line` (Int32, non-nullable), `end_line` (Int32, non-nullable), `content` (Utf8, non-nullable), `language` (Utf8, non-nullable), `symbol_name` (Utf8, nullable), `symbol_kind` (Utf8, nullable), `embedding` (FixedSizeList of Float32 with size 768, non-nullable — wrapped via `arrow_schema::DataType::FixedSizeList(Arc::new(Field::new("item", DataType::Float32, false)), 768)`), `token_count` (Int32, non-nullable), `file_hash` (Utf8, non-nullable), `indexed_at` (Timestamp(Microsecond, None), non-nullable). The exact `nullable` flags match the master-plan §12.2 string-vs-symbol_name distinction (`symbol_name`/`symbol_kind` may be empty for non-symbol chunks like docstring blocks). Rustdoc on the function cites §12.2 lines 1322-1336 verbatim and notes the 768-dim default for CodeRankEmbed (P2-W8-F02); a future ADR may grow this to 1024 for Qwen3-Embedding (P2-W8-F03 — line 1332 of the master plan documents both options).

Declare `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] #[non_exhaustive] pub enum BranchManagerError` with variants: (a) `#[error("branch path is not valid UTF-8: {path:?}")] NonUtf8Path { path: PathBuf }` (LanceDB requires UTF-8 URIs); (b) `#[error("lancedb operation failed: {source}")] Lance { #[from] source: lancedb::Error }` (auto-conversion from lancedb errors via `?`); (c) `#[error("arrow schema construction failed: {source}")] Arrow { #[from] source: arrow_schema::ArrowError }`; (d) `#[error("io error during branch directory operation: {source}")] Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error }`; (e) `#[error("parent branch '{parent}' has no vectors directory at {path:?}")] ParentNotFound { parent: String, path: PathBuf }` (clone-from-parent failure when source is missing); (f) `#[error("branch '{name}' has no vectors directory to archive at {path:?}")] BranchNotFound { name: String, path: PathBuf }` (archive failure when target is missing). The `#[non_exhaustive]` attribute follows the `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` invariant for libraries.

Declare `pub struct BranchTableInfo { pub branch: String, pub vectors_dir: PathBuf, pub table_count: usize }` (`#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]`) — returned by `create_branch_table` so callers can audit the post-creation state without re-opening the connection. `branch` is the SANITISED name (`/` → `-`), `vectors_dir` is the absolute path to the LanceDB connection root, `table_count` is the number of tables present after creation (1 for fresh creation, ≥ 1 for delta-clone where the parent had ≥ 1 table).

Declare `pub struct BranchManager { branches_root: PathBuf, archive_root: PathBuf }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone)]`. Constructor: `pub fn new(branches_root: impl Into<PathBuf>) -> Self { let branches_root = branches_root.into(); let archive_root = branches_root.join(ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME); Self { branches_root, archive_root } }`. Accessors: `pub fn branches_root(&self) -> &Path` (returns `&Path`, not `&PathBuf`, per Rust idiom); `pub fn archive_root(&self) -> &Path`; `pub fn branch_vectors_dir(&self, name: &str) -> PathBuf { self.branches_root.join(sanitise_branch_name(name)).join("vectors") }`. The internal `fn sanitise_branch_name(name: &str) -> String` mirrors `storage::sanitise_branch` — `name.replace('/', "-")` — with a leading `// Mirror of storage::sanitise_branch (private; intentional duplication so branch_manager.rs is self-contained — see DEC-NNNN if a sharing helper is later proposed)` comment. Mirror NOT shared to keep this WO single-file; cross-module sharing is a future cleanup with its own ADR.

Implement `pub async fn create_branch_table(&self, name: &str, parent: Option<&str>) -> Result<BranchTableInfo, BranchManagerError>` with this exact contract: (1) `let sanitised = sanitise_branch_name(name);` (2) `let target_branch_dir = self.branches_root.join(&sanitised);` (3) `let target_vectors = target_branch_dir.join("vectors");` (4) if let `Some(parent_name) = parent` { `let sanitised_parent = sanitise_branch_name(parent_name); let source_vectors = self.branches_root.join(&sanitised_parent).join("vectors");` if `!tokio::fs::try_exists(&source_vectors).await?` → `return Err(BranchManagerError::ParentNotFound { parent: sanitised_parent, path: source_vectors })`; perform recursive copy via a private async helper `copy_dir_recursive(&source_vectors, &target_vectors).await?` (uses `tokio::fs::create_dir_all` + `tokio::fs::read_dir` + per-entry copy/recurse — NO new crate dep; ~30 LOC) }. (5) `tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&target_vectors).await?` (idempotent; no-op if cloned). (6) `let uri = target_vectors.to_str().ok_or_else(|| BranchManagerError::NonUtf8Path { path: target_vectors.clone() })?;` (7) `let conn = lancedb::connect(uri).execute().await?;` (8) `let existing = conn.table_names().execute().await?;` (9) if `!existing.iter().any(|t| t == "code_chunks")` { `conn.create_empty_table("code_chunks", code_chunks_schema()).execute().await?;` } (10) re-list to compute table_count: `let final_tables = conn.table_names().execute().await?;` (11) `Ok(BranchTableInfo { branch: sanitised, vectors_dir: target_vectors, table_count: final_tables.len() })`. The function MUST be `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.daemon.branch_manager.create", level = "debug", skip(self), fields(branch = name, parent = ?parent))]` per master-plan §15.2 span naming `ucil.<layer>.<op>`.

Implement `pub async fn archive_branch_table(&self, name: &str) -> Result<PathBuf, BranchManagerError>` returning the absolute path of the archive target on success. Contract: (1) `let sanitised = sanitise_branch_name(name);` (2) `let source = self.branches_root.join(&sanitised);` (3) if `!tokio::fs::try_exists(&source).await?` → `return Err(BranchManagerError::BranchNotFound { name: sanitised, path: source });` (4) `let ts_micros = std::time::SystemTime::now().duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH).map(|d| d.as_micros()).unwrap_or_default();` (`unwrap_or_default` on the duration since UNIX_EPOCH is structurally safe — system clocks earlier than 1970 are not a runtime concern; the `Duration` zero-value gives a deterministic suffix on the rare clock skew); (5) `tokio::fs::create_dir_all(&self.archive_root).await?;` (6) `let target = self.archive_root.join(format!("{sanitised}-{ts_micros}"));` (7) `tokio::fs::rename(&source, &target).await?;` (atomic on same filesystem; preserves whole branch dir contents — vectors/, symbols.db, tags.lmdb, state.json — so the archive is a complete snapshot, not just the vectors). (8) `Ok(target)`. Tracing span: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.daemon.branch_manager.archive", level = "debug", skip(self), fields(branch = name))]`.

Implement the private async helper `async fn copy_dir_recursive(src: &Path, dst: &Path) -> Result<(), BranchManagerError>` using `Box::pin` recursion (since async recursion requires a heap-allocated future). Algorithm: (a) `tokio::fs::create_dir_all(dst).await?;` (b) `let mut entries = tokio::fs::read_dir(src).await?;` (c) `while let Some(entry) = entries.next_entry().await? { let src_path = entry.path(); let dst_path = dst.join(entry.file_name()); let ftype = entry.file_type().await?; if ftype.is_dir() { Box::pin(copy_dir_recursive(&src_path, &dst_path)).await?; } else if ftype.is_file() { tokio::fs::copy(&src_path, &dst_path).await?; } /* skip symlinks per master-plan §11.4 line 1090 'no symlink traversal in branch trees' */ }` (d) `Ok(())`. The function is private (no `pub` qualifier) and MUST NOT be re-exported. Box-pin recursion shape per `tokio::fs` async-recursion pattern (no new crate needed).

Add the frozen acceptance test `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_lancedb_per_branch()` at MODULE ROOT of `branch_manager.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The selector `branch_manager::test_lancedb_per_branch` resolves to `ucil_daemon::branch_manager::test_lancedb_per_branch` — same placement convention as `storage::test_two_tier_layout` at `storage.rs:267`. Test body (5 sub-assertions in order):  
  **Setup**: `let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("tmpdir"); let branches_root = tmp.path().join("branches"); tokio::fs::create_dir(&branches_root).await.expect("mkdir branches"); let mgr = BranchManager::new(&branches_root);`.  
  **(SA1) Create root branch + open table**: `let info = mgr.create_branch_table("main", None).await.expect("create main"); assert_eq!(info.branch, "main", "sanitised name preserved on no-slash input; got {:?}", info.branch); assert!(branches_root.join("main/vectors").exists(), "vectors dir must exist; got contents={:?}", std::fs::read_dir(&branches_root).unwrap().collect::<Vec<_>>()); let conn = lancedb::connect(branches_root.join("main/vectors").to_str().unwrap()).execute().await.expect("lancedb connect"); let tables = conn.table_names().execute().await.expect("list tables"); assert!(tables.iter().any(|t| t == "code_chunks"), "code_chunks must be present; got {:?}", tables); assert_eq!(info.table_count, 1, "fresh branch has exactly 1 table; got {}", info.table_count);`.  
  **(SA2) Clone-from-parent**: `let info2 = mgr.create_branch_table("feat/foo", Some("main")).await.expect("clone child"); assert_eq!(info2.branch, "feat-foo", "slash-sanitisation; got {:?}", info2.branch); assert!(branches_root.join("feat-foo/vectors").exists(), "child vectors dir must exist; tree dump: {:?}", walk_dir(&branches_root)); let conn2 = lancedb::connect(branches_root.join("feat-foo/vectors").to_str().unwrap()).execute().await.expect("lancedb child connect"); let tables2 = conn2.table_names().execute().await.expect("list child tables"); assert!(tables2.iter().any(|t| t == "code_chunks"), "clone preserved code_chunks; got {:?}", tables2);`.  
  **(SA3) Sanitisation invariant**: `assert!(!branches_root.join("feat/foo").exists(), "raw slashed path must not exist as a literal directory; got listing {:?}", std::fs::read_dir(&branches_root).unwrap().collect::<Vec<_>>());` (this catches a regression where `sanitise_branch_name` is bypassed in `create_branch_table`).  
  **(SA4) Archive roundtrip**: `let archive_path = mgr.archive_branch_table("feat/foo").await.expect("archive"); assert!(!branches_root.join("feat-foo").exists(), "original branch dir gone after archive; tree={:?}", walk_dir(&branches_root)); assert!(branches_root.join(".archive").exists(), "archive root created; tree={:?}", walk_dir(&branches_root)); let archive_entries: Vec<_> = std::fs::read_dir(branches_root.join(".archive")).unwrap().filter_map(|e| e.ok()).map(|e| e.file_name().to_string_lossy().into_owned()).collect(); assert!(archive_entries.iter().any(|n| n.starts_with("feat-foo-")), "archive contains feat-foo-<ts> entry; got {:?}", archive_entries); assert!(archive_path.starts_with(&branches_root), "returned path under branches_root; got {:?}", archive_path);`.  
  **(SA5) Archive-side connectability**: `let archived_vectors = archive_path.join("vectors"); assert!(archived_vectors.exists(), "archived vectors dir present; archive_path={:?}", archive_path); let conn3 = lancedb::connect(archived_vectors.to_str().unwrap()).execute().await.expect("lancedb archive connect"); let tables3 = conn3.table_names().execute().await.expect("list archive tables"); assert!(tables3.iter().any(|t| t == "code_chunks"), "archive preserves code_chunks for forensic queries; got {:?}", tables3);`.  
  **Helper `walk_dir`** (file-scope `fn walk_dir(p: &Path) -> Vec<String> { ... }`) recurses one level and produces a Vec of file/dir names — improves panic-message diagnostics so a verifier failure prints actual tree state. Use a small fixed-recursion-limit (say, 3 levels deep) to avoid pathological output.  
  All assertions follow the `assert_eq!(actual, expected, "<plain-English description>; got {:?}", actual)` shape per WO-0042 lessons line 92 + WO-0051 lessons line 405.

Update `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (a) adding `pub mod branch_manager;` BEFORE the existing `pub mod executor;` line at line 98 (alphabetical: `b < e`); (b) update the module-level `//!` preamble to add a new paragraph naming WO-0053 + P2-W7-F09 + master-plan §6.4 + §12.2 citations + the deferral note that production wiring of `BranchManager` into the daemon's startup / branch-detection paths is deferred to P2-W8-F04 (`LanceDB background chunk indexing`); (c) add a NEW re-export line `pub use branch_manager::{BranchManager, BranchManagerError, BranchTableInfo, code_chunks_schema, ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME};` immediately after the existing `pub use executor::{...}` line (alphabetical block ordering preserved). Verify post-edit via `grep -nE 'pub use branch_manager::' crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` returns 1 match listing all 5 symbols.

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F09.sh` per the standing convention (model on `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F02.sh` and `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F07.sh`). Script must: (a) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon branch_manager::test_lancedb_per_branch -- --nocapture` and tee stdout to `/tmp/test-WO-0053.log`; (b) match either `cargo test`'s `test result: ok. ... 0 failed` shape OR nextest's `... tests passed` shape via the tolerant alternation `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. .* 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests? passed'` (per WO-0042 / WO-0048 / WO-0051 lessons — same reusable shape); (c) on non-zero exit, emit `[FAIL] P2-W7-F09: <reason>` with a hint pointing at the assertion line + the WO + the master-plan §6.4 / §12.2 references; (d) `chmod +x scripts/verify/P2-W7-F09.sh` so the verifier can run it directly. Header comment block cites WO-0053 + P2-W7-F09 + the test selector verbatim. The script does NOT need a `cargo build --workspace` warm-up step (no negative-search/ignore-rule semantics asserted) but DOES include the `shellcheck` PATH-fallback pattern from WO-0044 onwards.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 — for `create_branch_table` mutation (M1: early-return Ok no-op), the body becomes `#[allow(unused_variables, clippy::needless_pass_by_value, clippy::let_unit_value)] { let _ = self; let _ = name; let _ = parent; Ok(BranchTableInfo { branch: sanitise_branch_name(name), vectors_dir: PathBuf::new(), table_count: 0 }) }`; for `archive_branch_table` mutation (M2: early-return Ok no-op), body becomes `#[allow(unused_variables, clippy::let_unit_value)] { let _ = self; let _ = name; Ok(self.archive_root.clone()) }` — the returned path is a sentinel value that fails SA4's `archive_path.starts_with(&branches_root)` because `archive_root` is itself under `branches_root` so the root-prefix check passes BUT the `branches_root.join("feat-foo")` still exists, panicking SA4's first assertion; for clone-skip mutation (M3: parent block neutered), the `if let Some(parent_name) = parent` block becomes `if let Some(_) = parent { /* skip the copy step entirely */ }` — fresh empty `code_chunks` table is created in the child but parent's table is not propagated; verifier asserts SA2's `tables2.iter().any(|t| t == "code_chunks")` STILL passes (table is created fresh) BUT a TIGHTER assertion on table_count would catch this. Document this nuance in the mutation entry: M3 may NOT panic SA2 directly because the child IS given an empty table; instead the WO documents M3 as a 'soft regression — tests still pass'. The verifier MAY extend with M3a: skip-clone AND skip-create-empty (so neither path produces a table) → SA2 panics on `tables2.iter().any(|t| t == "code_chunks")` returning false. Document BOTH the literal sed AND runtime-only variants in `acceptance` mutation entries (AC18 / AC19 / AC20).

**Pre-flight rustdoc grep before pushing** (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown`): `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` — every uppercase identifier in rustdoc paragraphs MUST be wrapped in backticks (`` `BranchManager` ``, `` `LanceDB` ``, `` `UNIX_EPOCH` ``, etc.). The crate sets `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]`; use plain backticks NOT shorthand `[Foo]` intra-doc links unless the link is fully-qualified (`[`crate::executor::G1Source`]`).

**Operator-readable assertion messages** (per WO-0051 lessons line 405): every `assert_eq!` / `assert!` in `test_lancedb_per_branch` follows the shape `assert_eq!(actual, expected, "<plain-English description>; got {:?}", actual)`. The `walk_dir` helper makes tree-dump diagnostics cheap to include in panic messages without inflating test LOC.

**Commit ladder — 5 commits** per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` and DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out:  
  (1) `build(daemon): add lancedb + arrow workspace deps for per-branch vector store` (~25 LOC: 3 workspace deps + 3 `[dependencies]` lines), 
  (2) `feat(daemon): add BranchManager skeleton with code_chunks schema and error type` (~80 LOC: module preamble, `BranchManagerError`, `code_chunks_schema`, `BranchTableInfo`, `BranchManager::new` + accessors + `sanitise_branch_name` + `ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME`), 
  (3) `feat(daemon): implement BranchManager::create_branch_table with delta-clone from parent` (~80 LOC: `create_branch_table` + `copy_dir_recursive` private helper — both lines together because they share the recursion contract; soft-target ~50 LOC overage authorised by DEC-0005 module-coherence — splitting `copy_dir_recursive` into a separate commit produces dead-code intermediate state), 
  (4) `feat(daemon): implement BranchManager::archive_branch_table with timestamped archive paths` (~30 LOC: `archive_branch_table` + tracing spans), 
  (5) `test(daemon): add branch_manager::test_lancedb_per_branch covering 5 lifecycle sub-assertions` (~120 LOC test + ~25 LOC `walk_dir` helper; over the 50 LOC soft target — DEC-0005 carve-out: 5 sub-assertions form one coherent regression-guard for the feature contract; splitting would either (a) introduce a transient state where SA1+SA2 pass without SA4 verifying the archive invariant, OR (b) duplicate the temp-dir setup across 5 commits), 
  (6) [optional] `feat(verify): add scripts/verify/P2-W7-F09.sh end-to-end` (~40 LOC shell + chmod), 
  (7) [optional] `docs(daemon): expand lib.rs preamble with WO-0053 + P2-W7-F09 references` (~10-15 LOC rustdoc on the module-level `//!` block, preserving prior paragraphs and adding one new paragraph naming the F09 method-pair + master-plan §6.4 / §12.2 citations + the F04 production-wiring deferral). 
  Each commit signed via the conventional `Phase: 2 / Feature: P2-W7-F09 / Work-order: WO-0053` trailer. The lib.rs re-export line lands in commit (5) bundled with the test (cumulative-debt-avoidance carve-out per WO-0042 → WO-0048 lessons — re-exports land in the SAME WO that introduces them, NOT deferred).
scope_out: **No production wiring** of `BranchManager` into the daemon's startup, session-creation, or branch-detection code paths. Master-plan §6.4 places the `Branch index manager` adjacent to `Session manager` (line 144), but the production wiring (creating a per-branch LanceDB table on first session against a previously-unseen branch, archiving on branch deletion detection) is deferred to P2-W8-F04 (`LanceDB background chunk indexing`) per master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1788 ('LanceDB integration: background indexing, incremental updates, per-branch stores'). F09's contract is the standalone `BranchManager` API + the unit test verifying its lifecycle semantics; F04 (sibling WO) wires it into the daemon's startup path. `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, `lifecycle.rs`, `session_manager.rs`, `executor.rs`, `startup.rs`, `main.rs` are all in `forbidden_paths`.

**No actual vector data ingestion**. The test creates EMPTY `code_chunks` tables and verifies their presence; no row inserts, no embedding generation, no chunking. The full ingestion pipeline (chunking via tree-sitter boundaries → ONNX inference via CodeRankEmbed → LanceDB row insert) is exclusively P2-W8 territory (F02 through F08).

**No use of `lancedb`'s `delta` / `replicate` / `merge` features** (if such APIs exist in 0.10). The 'delta indexing from parent branches' phrase from the feature description is implemented as a directory recursive-copy at the filesystem level — preserves the parent's already-indexed Lance dataset files byte-for-byte, then subsequent indexing only re-processes changed files (per the `file_hash` column in the §12.2 schema, which P2-W8-F04 will populate). This design choice is documented in the module-level rustdoc.

**No symlink traversal** in `copy_dir_recursive`. Per master-plan §11.4 line 1090, branch trees are pure file/directory hierarchies; symlinks (if encountered via stash/worktree edge cases) are skipped. The implementation uses `entry.file_type().await?.is_file()` + `is_dir()` and SKIPS anything that isn't both — no `is_symlink()` follow.

**No GPU-only / Python-only LanceDB features.** `Cargo.toml`'s `lancedb = { version = "0.10", default-features = false }` is critical — full features pull in Python bindings, GPU search backends, and a multi-GB compile graph; ucil-daemon only needs the async Rust client. The executor MUST verify via `cargo tree -p ucil-daemon` that the dep tree does NOT include `pyo3`, `cuda`, or any Python-FFI crate.

**No new ADR**. DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits up to ~200 LOC for the test commit specifically), DEC-0011 (`test_support::env_guard` PATH mutex — NOT applicable here because `test_lancedb_per_branch` does NOT spawn `git` and does NOT mutate `PATH`; the existing `branch_manager` module is filesystem-only). If a NEW pattern surfaces (e.g. lancedb's API requires a different schema-construction pattern, or async recursion via `Box::pin` triggers an unexpected `clippy::large_futures` lint), STOP and write an ADR before working around.

**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` (M1, M2, M3 per scope_in[14]) are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 / 0047 / 0048 / 0050 / 0051 / 0052, now 10 consecutive cleared).

**No changes to `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`, `tests/**`, `tests/integration/**`** — F09 is single-crate (`ucil-daemon`), single-source-file (NEW `branch_manager.rs`). Same shape as WO-0048 (single-file `executor.rs` extension) but applied to a NEW source file.

**No changes to existing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/` files except `lib.rs`.** `executor.rs`, `server.rs`, `plugin_manager.rs`, `lifecycle.rs`, `storage.rs`, `watcher.rs`, `priority_queue.rs`, `startup.rs`, `text_search.rs`, `understand_code.rs`, `session_ttl.rs`, `session_manager.rs`, `test_support.rs`, `main.rs`, `bin/**` are all in `forbidden_paths`. The ONLY edit to existing source is the additive `pub mod branch_manager;` + re-export line + preamble paragraph in `lib.rs`.
**No `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, `commented-out assertions`, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

**No mock of `lancedb::Connection`, `tokio::fs`, `arrow_schema::Schema`, or `tempfile::TempDir`.** The test uses real `lancedb::connect` against a real `tempfile::TempDir` — no shim, no fake. LanceDB is a critical dep per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` (no mocks of Serena, LSP, SQLite, LanceDB, Docker); WO-0046 lessons line 248 confirms tempfile-backed LanceDB is the correct approach. The `BranchManager` is UCIL-owned state — mocking it would defeat the test's purpose.
**No `git commit --no-verify`, `--amend` after push, or force-push** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.
acceptance: AC01: `Cargo.toml` workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` declares `lancedb`, `arrow-array`, `arrow-schema` — verified via `grep -nE '^lancedb = |^arrow-array = |^arrow-schema = ' Cargo.toml | wc -l` returning `3`.

AC02: `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` declares the three new crates as workspace deps — verified via `grep -nE '^lancedb\.workspace = true|^arrow-array\.workspace = true|^arrow-schema\.workspace = true' crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml | wc -l` returning `3`.

AC03: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` exists and declares `pub struct BranchManager` — verified via `test -f crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs && grep -nE '^pub struct BranchManager' crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs`.

AC04: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` declares `pub async fn create_branch_table` and `pub async fn archive_branch_table` — verified via `grep -nE '^[[:space:]]*pub async fn create_branch_table|^[[:space:]]*pub async fn archive_branch_table' crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs | wc -l` returning `2`.

AC05: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` declares `pub fn code_chunks_schema` and `pub const ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME` — verified via `grep -nE '^pub fn code_chunks_schema|^pub const ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME' crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs | wc -l` returning `2`.

AC06: `test_lancedb_per_branch` is at MODULE ROOT (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007 — verified via `grep -nE '^async fn test_lancedb_per_branch\(' crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs | wc -l` returning `1` (un-indented, column 0).
AC07: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` declares `pub mod branch_manager;` — verified via `grep -nE '^pub mod branch_manager;' crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs | wc -l` returning `1`.

AC08: `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` re-exports the 5 new symbols — verified via `grep -nE 'pub use branch_manager::\{.*BranchManager.*BranchManagerError.*BranchTableInfo.*code_chunks_schema.*ARCHIVE_DIR_NAME.*\}' crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs | wc -l` returning `1` (order may vary; the 5 symbol names must all appear on the same line).
AC09: `cargo build -p ucil-daemon --tests --quiet 2>&1 | tee /tmp/build-WO-0053.log` exits 0.

AC10: Frozen acceptance — `cargo test -p ucil-daemon branch_manager::test_lancedb_per_branch 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-WO-0053.log && grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests? run: 1 passed|1 tests? passed' /tmp/test-WO-0053.log`.

AC11: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon storage::test_two_tier_layout 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-storage-WO-0053.log && grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests? run: 1 passed|1 tests? passed' /tmp/test-storage-WO-0053.log` — Phase 1 regression guard (P1-W2-F06 storage layout test stays green).
AC12: `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-ws-WO-0053.log && ! grep -Eq 'test result: FAILED' /tmp/test-ws-WO-0053.log` — workspace stays green per phase-2 standing rule §4.

AC13: `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy-WO-0053.log && ! grep -q '^error' /tmp/clippy-WO-0053.log` — clippy clean under `#![deny(warnings)]`, `#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]`.
AC14: `cargo fmt --check` — workspace fmt clean.

AC15: `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps 2>&1 | tee /tmp/doc-WO-0053.log && ! grep -Eq '(error|warning):' /tmp/doc-WO-0053.log` — rustdoc clean under `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]`. New methods have `# Examples` blocks.
AC16: `bash scripts/verify/P2-W7-F09.sh` exits 0 — frozen feature script per `feature-list.json`.

AC17: NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, or commented-out assertions in `branch_manager.rs` — verified via `! grep -nE '#\[ignore\]|todo!\(|unimplemented!\(|//[[:space:]]*assert' crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs`.

AC18: **Mutation M1 (literal sed)** — neuter `create_branch_table` body to early-return `Ok(BranchTableInfo { branch: sanitise_branch_name(name), vectors_dir: PathBuf::new(), table_count: 0 })` (prepend at the function-body start, comment out remainder) → re-run AC10 → expect FAILURE at SA1 panic line (panic message: 'vectors dir must exist'). **Runtime-only variant**: prefix the function body with `#[allow(unused_variables, clippy::needless_pass_by_value, clippy::let_unit_value)] { let _ = self; let _ = name; let _ = parent; return Ok(BranchTableInfo { branch: sanitise_branch_name(name), vectors_dir: PathBuf::new(), table_count: 0 }); }` and `#[allow(unreachable_code)]` on the remainder — same expected fail-line. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` → AC10 green again.

AC19: **Mutation M2 (literal sed)** — neuter `archive_branch_table` body to `return Ok(self.archive_root.clone());` (prepend at function-body start) → re-run AC10 → expect FAILURE at SA4 panel (panic message: 'original branch dir gone after archive'). **Runtime-only variant**: prefix body with `#[allow(unused_variables)] { let _ = self; let _ = name; return Ok(self.archive_root.clone()); }` and `#[allow(unreachable_code)]` on the remainder. Restore: `git checkout`.

AC20: **Mutation M3 (literal sed)** — neuter the `if let Some(parent_name) = parent { copy_dir_recursive(...); ... }` block to skip the directory copy entirely (replace block body with `let _ = parent_name;`) → re-run AC10 → MAY pass SA2's `code_chunks` listing (table created fresh in child) but FAILS the tighter assertion that `info2.table_count` equals the parent's table count when M3a is applied (M3a: also skip the `create_empty_table` step in the child path → SA2 panics on `tables2.iter().any(|t| t == \"code_chunks\")` returning false). The verifier MAY apply M3 (soft-regression case) or M3a (hard-regression case) per their judgment; document the choice in the verification report. Restore: `git checkout`.

AC21: Module-level `//!` rustdoc preamble in `branch_manager.rs` mentions `code_chunks` + cites master-plan §6.4 line 144 + §12.2 — verified via `grep -E 'code_chunks.*§6\.4|§6\.4.*code_chunks|line 144|§12\.2' crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs | wc -l` returning at least `2` (one for §6.4, one for §12.2). Documentation sign-post per WO-0045/0048 lessons (load-bearing preamble discipline).

AC22: All commit subject lines ≤ 70 chars per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md`. Verified via `git log main...HEAD --pretty='%s' | awk '{ if (length($0) > 70) { print "too-long: " $0; exit 1 } }'` returning success.

AC23: `git diff --name-only main...HEAD` (3-dot diff per WO-0048 lessons line 337 — now 7 consecutive WOs using this) returns ONLY: `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`, `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F09.sh`, `ucil-build/work-orders/0053-ready-for-review.md`. Cargo.toml
crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml
crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs
crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs
scripts/verify/P2-W7-F09.sh
ucil-build/work-orders/0053-ready-for-review.md' | sort)" ]`.

AC24: `cargo tree -p ucil-daemon 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'pyo3|cuda|libcuda|libpython' && exit 1 || exit 0` — confirms no GPU/Python features leaked in via lancedb. (Inverted exit because grep success means a forbidden dep was found.)
